arts.ttl: Stoke City boss, the Jose Mourinho playbook, quick fixes and major challenges
arts.sum: If you don’t have the forward player doing that, it’s a hell of a lot of work when you’re defending deep. From the start against Hull you could see that Stoke had done work on pressing when the opposition had the ball in their defensive third.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick considers ringing the changes for misfiring Middlesbrough against Stoke City
arts.sum: With Boro struggling in attack as much as they have been, however, with only one league goal scored in open play in their first six games, there have also been calls for a more drastic formation or stylistic change. Carrick has always said that the size of his Boro squad would be no option.

arts.ttl: ‘Amazing player’ – Stoke City boss sheds light on private talks with 22-year-old
arts.sum: Stoke City boss Narcis Pelach has spoken of his expectations for his players with Million Manhoef singled out… The post ‘Amazing player’ – Stoke City boss sheds light on private talks with 22-year-old appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: New Stoke City boss's promise on style of play can be good news for Middlesbrough
arts.sum: While acknowledging his side might have to deal with being without the ball for larger periods in the game, and will therefore have to try and be compact defensively, he is also keen that his players show perhaps more attacking endeavour than most of Boro's opponents this season. Narcis Pelach says he wants his Stoke City side to be aggressive and brave as he tries to establish his way on his new players - and that can be good news for Middlesbrough this weekend.

arts.ttl: Narcis Pelach's Stoke City fitness plan and urgency of results despite eye on long-term
arts.sum: “I’m happy with the performance the other day, not happy with how it finished and we need to win games to get the confidence up but I think everyone around the building is feeling that we’re going in another direction, things are going to be done in a different way and it’s about having good body language and positivity to get there as quickly as possible knowing it’s going to be a long journey.” Pelach is taking Stoke to Middlesbrough in the Championship this weekend (Saturday, 3pm) having seen the team post their highest running statistics for any game so far this term in his first game in charge last week against Hull.

arts.ttl: Stoke City summer signing remains out of action for Middlesbrough trip
arts.sum: Stoke City defender Ashley Phillips remains sidelined this weekend, as detailed in a report by StokeOnTrentLive. Stoke City… The post Stoke City summer signing remains out of action for Middlesbrough trip appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Riley McGree caution explained with Middlesbrough star 'chomping at the bit' for return
arts.sum: Michael Carrick says Riley McGree is chomping at the bit to make his return from injury for Middlesbrough, with the Boro boss explaining his reasoning for taking a cautious approach with his comeback from injury. With those efforts and a full pre-season still proving futile in helping the 25-year-old avoid the early injury this season, the Boro boss is now being cautious with McGree's latest comeback, in the hope that this time he's back for the long-term.

arts.ttl: Stoke City summer signing remains out of action for Middlesbrough trip
arts.sum: Stoke City defender Ashley Phillips remains sidelined this weekend, as detailed in a report by StokeOnTrentLive. Stoke City… The post Stoke City summer signing remains out of action for Middlesbrough trip appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Narcis Pelach press conference in full as Stoke head to Middlesbrough
arts.sum: I’m happy with the performance the other day, not happy with how it finished and we need to win games to get the confidence up but I think everyone around the building is feeling that we’re going in another direction, things are going to be done in a different way and it’s about having good body language and positivity to get there as quickly as possible knowing it’s going to be a long journey. Bad things happen in a game and that’s always going to happen for all of us, not just Stoke City but every team in the Championship or the world.

arts.ttl: Inside Michael Carrick's press conference with the Middlesbrough boss bullish under pressure
arts.sum: I think you can see that in the way the games are and the time we’re spending in and around the opposition box compared to our own box, the chances we’re having and things like that. It came after Carrick noted after the Sunderland defeat that many teams were sitting deeper and playing a different way to usual against them this season.
